items.title: NT government opens door to private development in four national parks

children.content: The Northern Territory government says opening up national parks to private investors will unlock their "tourism potential", but an expert fears the move could mean less access for locals and budget-conscious visitors.

gallery.altText: Wangi Falls in Litchfield National Park

thumbnail.altText: Wangi Falls in Litchfield National Park

items.title: Captain Cook Shopping Centre sold and signs removed in town's new chapter

children.content: British explorer James Cook never visited Nhulunbuy on his voyages, but his namesake shopping complex stood there for decades — until now.

image.altText: Two men remove a sign from a fence.

items.title: Rural suburb flagged as home for proposed $40b 'hyperscale' data centre

children.content: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

image.altText: An AI generated image of the outside of the proposed facility.

items.title: NT to become last jurisdiction to legalise voluntary assisted dying

children.content: The Northern Territory is set to make voluntary assisted dying legal in August. The ABC understands all 17 Country Liberal Party government members will vote in favour of the Rights of the Terminally Ill Act.

image.altText: VAD brochure

items.title: Woolworths charged over NT crash between forklift, motorcyclist

children.content: Woolworths has been hit with a string of workplace safety charges after one of the supermarket giant's forklift operators allegedly struck a motorcyclist on a public road, causing "serious injuries", in 2025.

image.altText: Emergency services surround a crashed red motorcycle on a public street.

items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby child protection review finds departmental failings

children.content: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, an interim report into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman wearing a police uniform

articles.title: Protest held ahead of NT child protection laws debate

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is debating controversial child protection laws that have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: A large crowd of protesters standing on a grassed area, holding signs.

gallery.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

thumbnail.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

items.title: Police confirm investigation involving incident with NT administrator

children.content: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and jacket behind a microphone

items.title: PM backs bid for future NT AFL team, no promises on funding

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

image.altText: The Tiwi Islands footy team with Anthony Albanese.

items.title: Sixty bikes stolen from Darwin community organisation

children.content: About 60 donated bicycles have been stolen from the Darwin Men's Shed storage site, delaying the organisation's plans to support remote communities.

gallery.altText: A man in a blue shirt in a workshop

thumbnail.altText: A man in a blue shirt in a workshop

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Critical breeding breakthrough welcome news for lovers of purple passionfruit

children.content: An unpalatable future has been averted for Australians who love purple-skinned passionfruit, with new research ensuring better prospects for the delicious fruit.

image.altText: A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

items.title: Labor to extend paid placements to more care industry students

children.content: Anthony Albanese will announce an expansion of the Commonwealth's "paid prac" scheme at Labor's national conference. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese

items.title: OpenAI model goes rogue during testing and hacks startup

children.content: OpenAI has announced one of its AI models broke containment during testing before hacking into another AI startup.

image.altText: Sam Altman standing in front of the OpenAI logo.

items.title: The 'fairy gold dust passport' more Australians are chasing

children.content: Australians are racing to secure Irish citizenship, with new figures showing applications have more than doubled in the past three years as people seek the right to live, work and study across the EU.

Three Irish passports layed out next to one Australian passport

items.title: Labor's members to debate gas and gambling

children.content: Anthony Albanese may have been a keen participant in robust conference debates in years gone by but is now using his authority to enforce a much more controlled process.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: At the CPAC London conference, someone fell asleep behind my colleague

children.content: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

image.altText: A woman and a man speaking.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach nuclear power deal

children.content: Israeli officials and US politicians from both parties have voiced concern that a civilian nuclear project in Saudi Arabia could be used to eventually develop nuclear weapons.

image.altText: Donald Trump holding hands with Mohammed Bin Salman during a meeting in the Oval Office.

items.title: Trump threatens Iranian power plants, bridges if ships attacked in Hormuz

children.content: US strikes have already widened from the south to western and central areas of Iran in recent days.

image.altText: A woman in Islamic dress walks on a street under a billboard showing a MAGA hat and the words "Hey terrorist. Get ready to die"

items.title: From Mumbai to New York, young Indians gather in unprecedented movement

children.content: A student who started a youth uprising across India is fast becoming the biggest challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 12-year reign.

items.title: ASX set to open higher as Tesla shares plunge

children.content: The Australian share market is set to open in the green after Tesla shares plunging on cash flow news. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Nintendo raised prices due to US tariffs. Now, it doesn't want to pass on refunds

children.content: Nintendo has argued it has no duty to pass its refunds onto US consumers. It's the latest in a lengthy tariff fight.

image.altText: A handheld gaming device.

items.title: China demands Australia stop 'inciting confrontation' over South China Sea

children.content: China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs has demanded Australia stop "exaggerating tensions and inciting confrontation" over the South China Sea after Penny Wong warned Beijing against "destabilising" conduct in the region.

image.altText: Penny Wong and Wang Yi shake hands.

items.title: How we got to Glasgow 2026 and what the Commonwealth Games will look like

children.content: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

articles.title: Kennedy arrives early to lead Australia into Glasgow opening ceremony

children.content: Reigning Commonwealth Games gold medallists Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge will carry the Australian flag and King's baton at the Glasgow 2026 opening ceremony.

image.altText: Nina Kennedy holds the Australian flag behind her.

articles.title: Weightlifter puts 'best foot forward' for Comm Games

children.content: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

image.altText: Kiana Elliott at a gym, bending down with her hands on weights as she prepares to lift them. She is dressed in a blue t-shirt

gallery.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

thumbnail.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

items.title: How One Nation is winning over the bush

children.content: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?

items.title: Hobart council snack-saga motion to be heard behind closed doors

items.title: Prince George turns 13 as he heads to Eton

children.content: Prince George, second in line to the throne, has turned 13 as he readies to follow in his father Prince William's footsteps and start at the prestigious Eton College.

items.title: AFL's first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

children.content: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk Header image

items.title: Star footballer's protest over transfer leads to training cancellation

children.content: Star Wolverhampton striker Tolu Arokodare allegedly refuses to leave the training pitch in protest over his transfer to a Turkish club.

image.altText: A man trains on a football pitch.

items.title: Victorian teachers begin second major strike as pay talks stall

children.content: State school teachers and support staff will rally in Melbourne's CBD after walking off the job following the rejection of a government pay increase of 28 per cent.

image.altText: Teachers cheer during speeches at the strike rally in Melbourne.

items.title: Queensland cattle farmers eye $100m move to crops

children.content: A $5.3 million investment aims to expand cropping across North West Queensland, with ambitious targets for cotton, grain and pulses in traditionally cattle country.

image.altText: Lucas Findley stands with visitors among a mature cotton crop at Etta Plains Station, north of Julia Creek.

items.title: Rock Posters proud to capture artists' 'blood and tears' in billboards

children.content: The iconic Melbourne printing factory has worked with international pop divas and rock legends to enhance the city's live music scene for 40 years.
